name: Hot Sour Hunan Veggies
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons fermented black beans, coarsely chopped (Chinese)
3/4 pound cauliflower florets, cut into walnut-sized pieces
1/2 pound peeled carrots, cut diagonally into 1/8 inch coins
1 pound slender green and golden zucchini, cut into 1/4 inch rounds
1 tablespoon cornstarch dissolved in 1 1/2 tablespoons water
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine ginger, garlic, black beans, and red pepper flakes. Cover and set aside.
2. In a second bowl, blend vegetable stock, soy sauce, vinegar, and sugar together.
3. Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Fill a large bowl with ice water.
4. Blanch cauliflower for 1 minute, then plunge into the ice water. Repeat with remaining vegetables, letting the water return to a boil before adding more.
5. Blanch carrots for 15 seconds and zucchini for 5 seconds. Drain well and place in separate bowls.
6. Heat a wok or large heavy skillet over high heat until very hot. Add about 2 tablespoons of water and swirl to glaze the pan.
7. Reduce heat to moderately high, then add the ginger mixture and stir gently until fragrant, about 20-30 seconds.
8. Add cauliflower and toss for 2 minutes. Add carrots and toss for 1 1/2 minutes, then add zucchini and toss for 30 seconds.
9. Stir in the vegetable stock mixture and add to the pan, stirring to combine. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook until vegetables are crisp-tender, about 30-60 seconds.
10. Stir the cornstarch mixture to blend, then add to the pan and stir until the sauce turns glossy and slightly thick, about 10-20 seconds.
11. Serve immediately on a heated platter, garnished with scallion rings and accompanied by noodles or rice. Serves 4 to 6.
